title: 'No space left on device' installing a package -- but there's no huge log anywhere
description_md:
Installing or upgrading any package on this box now fails with `No space left on device`. There's no runaway log file and no obvious culprit in `/var/log`. This box hasn't had its package cache cleaned in months. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find what's actually eating the space, and free it up the right way.

You're done when the filesystem backing `/var/cache/apt/archives` has a healthy amount of free space again (several MB).
